verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Dirty-shutdown recovery works; WAL/replay validated; No corruption; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the engine and receipt absent (5/5 failing), green at HEAD (5/5)
gate_result.recovery_evidence: scripts/dirty-shutdown-recovery.mjs executed the real drill against the live canonical cluster: 500 committed sentinel rows written to a dedicated drill database, kill -9 on the live postmaster (true crash — no shutdown checkpoint), port 5432 confirmed refusing, stale postmaster.pid observed as the dirty marker, then recovery through the sanctioned reattach path (startServicesOrdered(productionServices)) with health-gated return to accepting connections.
gate_result.replay_evidence: Validated from the cluster's own log at the recorded pre-crash offset: 'database system was interrupted; last known up at 2026-07-22 09:33:08', 'database system was not properly shut down; automatic recovery in progress', 'redo starts at 0/119DC7E0', 'redo done at 0/11E20EF8', 'database system is ready to accept connections'. The always-on spec re-verifies these lines live from the real logfile (tail-read) on every suite run.
gate_result.corruption_evidence: Sentinel md5 checksum identical across the crash (cbeb4add42a023bb63560e3cbd78dcc5 before and after), 500/500 rows read back, redb-plane probe file byte-identical, durable plane checks passing. Rollback honored: the drill database was dropped and the redb probe removed; the durable receipt at meta/var/xdg-data/lifeos/crash-drill/receipt.json is the retained evidence.
gate_result.honest_notes: The drill executed twice: the first invocation completed its crash cycle but its receipt pipeline broke in shell plumbing before writing; the second run performed a full clean cycle and wrote the receipt. Both crash cycles recovered cleanly (two 'not properly shut down' recovery blocks in the log) — corroborating rather than weakening the result. Separately, the drill surfaced that the cluster logfile is 1.79 GB and unrotated — recorded as GitKB incidents/postgres-logfile-unrotated with a config fix path; engine and spec tail-read by offset, never wholesale.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The kill -9, WAL replay, and integrity verification all ran against the real PostgreSQL 17.10 + RuVector canonical cluster on its real durable datadir — not a fixture; the recovery lines quoted are the production cluster's own.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-133 is complete: envelope-level dirty-shutdown recovery is proven on the real canonical cluster — a true kill -9 crash with confirmed downtime and dirty marker, recovery through the sanctioned reattach path, WAL replay validated from the cluster's own redo lines, and byte-identical sentinel data across the crash with the redb plane intact and rollback honored.
